baseline.observed: The UI converted limit input with Number(limit) and sent it directly to scan_prompts.
The input declared min 100 and max 100000, but click-time parsing did not enforce positive whole-number bounds before IPC.

observed_weaknesses: Invalid limit values could reach the Tauri boundary instead of producing an immediate UI error.
The UI minimum did not align with the backend/CLI positive limit behavior.

selected_candidate.name: UI scan limit validation
selected_candidate.reason: Fail fast in the user-facing control before IPC and keep frontend/backend limit semantics aligned.
selected_candidate.success_metric: TypeScript build and full check pass after adding client-side positive whole-number validation.

safety_controls: The change is limited to src/App.tsx.
The scan limit remains optional when the field is empty.
Error formatting was normalized without changing scan result rendering.
npm run build and npm run check were rerun.
